title: Emergency Guide: What to Do If Your Cat Swallowed a Sewing Needle
meta_desc: If your cat swallowed a sewing needle, this is a high-priority emergency. Learn the immediate steps to take and why you must see a vet now.
content_html:
TL;DR: Swallowing a sewing needle is a life-threatening emergency for cats that requires immediate veterinary intervention. Do not induce vomiting, as the needle can cause severe internal punctures or become lodged in the chest.

What happens when a cat swallows a sewing needle?

When a cat swallows a sewing needle, it is classified as a sharp foreign body ingestion. Often, cats are attracted to the thread attached to the needle, and their backwards-facing tongue barbs make it difficult for them to spit the object out once they start swallowing. This situation is extremely dangerous because the needle can puncture the esophagus, stomach, or intestines.

Is it an emergency if my cat swallows a needle?

  • Urgency Level: High. This is a life-threatening medical emergency.
  • Do not wait for symptoms to appear.
  • Do not attempt to induce vomiting, as the needle can cause even more damage or pierce the chest cavity on the way back up.
  • Contact your nearest emergency veterinary clinic immediately for an X-ray to locate the object.

How can a photo of the needle help my veterinarian?

  • Take a clear photo of an identical needle or the spool of thread to help the vet determine the size, shape, and type.
  • Provide details on the length and material of any thread attached, as this can cause the intestines to bunch up and lead to rapid tissue death.
  • Sharing these details helps the veterinary team prepare for the specific surgical or endoscopic retrieval process.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

If a cat has swallowed a sewing needle, endoscopic removal via a flexible endoscope and forceps is indicated if possible; a rigid endoscope can be used if a flexible scope is unavailable, but care must be taken to prevent lacerations or perforations. Alternatively, the needle may be pushed into the stomach, where it can hopefully pass or be removed via gastrotomy. Surgery is indicated if a perforation has occurred or the foreign body cannot be removed via endoscopy. Radiographs should be taken to assess the location of the needle and to exclude the presence of multiple foreign bodies.

Chapter: Gastroenterology

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 373)

faqs.question: Can I wait to see if the needle passes in the stool?
faqs.answer: No. The risk of the needle perforating the intestinal wall or migrating into the abdominal cavity or vital organs is too high. Professional medical intervention is required immediately.

faqs.question: What if I see the thread hanging out of my cat's mouth?
faqs.answer: Do not pull the thread. Pulling it can cause the needle to hook deeper into tissue or cause the thread to cut through the delicate lining of the esophagus or intestines. Leave it alone and get to a vet.

faqs.question: How will the vet remove the needle?
faqs.answer: Depending on the location found via X-ray, the vet may use an endoscope (a camera with a grabber) to pull the needle out through the mouth while the cat is sedated, or they may require emergency abdominal surgery.
